name: Riello from Embalse de Selga de Ordás – circular by Carrizo de la Ribera and Tapia de la Ribera
name_original: Riello desde Embalse de Selga de Ordás – circular por Carrizo de la Ribera y Tapia de la Ribera
region_intro: Road cycling around Carrocera, located in the province of León, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features a mix of flatter sections, particularly towards the south, alongside mountainous areas to the north and west, including the foothills of the Picos de Europa. Cyclists can expect well-maintained asphalt roads winding through valleys, forests, and cultivated hills, with significant elevation gains in some areas. The landscape is characterized by varied natural environments, including reservoirs and dramatic gorges. Best road cycling routes in Carrocera The most popular road cycling route is Barrios de Luna Reservoir and La Robla Loop, a 46.8 miles (75.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route features challenging climbs and scenic views around the Embalse Barrios de Luna. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Riello from Embalse de Selga de Ordás – circular by Carrizo de la Ribera and Tapia de la Ribera, a moderate 43.3 miles (69.8 km) path. This route traverses scenic countryside and passes through local villages. Local road cyclists also love the Villadalte – Embalse de Selga de Ordás loop from Embalse de Selga de Ordás, a 44.4 miles (71.5 km) trail leading through cultivated hills and past the Embalse de Selga de Ordás, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Carrocera is defined by mountains, forests, and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through valleys to difficult ascents. The routes in Carrocera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 8 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carrocera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Carrocera?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Carrocera. These include 18 moderate routes and 11 difficult routes, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Carrocera?
A: Road cycling around Carrocera offers diverse terrain. You'll find a mix of flatter sections, especially towards the south, alongside more mountainous areas to the north and west, including the foothills of the Picos de Europa. Expect well-maintained asphalt roads winding through valleys, forests, and cultivated hills, with significant elevation gains on some routes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Carrocera?
A: Yes, Carrocera offers several challenging road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042316978/barrios-de-luna-reservoir-and-la-robla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrios de Luna Reservoir and La Robla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 46.8-mile (75.2 km) route featuring demanding climbs and scenic views around the Embalse Barrios de Luna.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Carrocera?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural scenery and points of interest. You might encounter the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5548992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrios de Luna Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or even the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Camparona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Carrocera?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046685988/riello-from-embalse-de-selga-de-ordas-circular-by-carrizo-de-la-ribera-and-tapia-de-la-ribera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riello from Embalse de Selga de Ordás – circular by Carrizo de la Ribera and Tapia de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses scenic countryside and local villages. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5341733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villadalte – Embalse de Selga de Ordás loop from Embalse de Selga de Ordás&lt;/a&gt;, leading through cultivated hills.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Carrocera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from quiet valley roads to challenging mountain ascents, and the beautiful natural landscapes, including reservoirs and forests.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional Spanish villages?
A: Yes, many routes offer cultural immersion by passing through charming local villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046685988/riello-from-embalse-de-selga-de-ordas-circular-by-carrizo-de-la-ribera-and-tapia-de-la-ribera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riello from Embalse de Selga de Ordás – circular by Carrizo de la Ribera and Tapia de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; route is a good example, allowing you to experience the local atmosphere.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gains for training?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous areas to the north and west of Carrocera, including the foothills of the Picos de Europa, feature significant elevation gains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042316978/barrios-de-luna-reservoir-and-la-robla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrios de Luna Reservoir and La Robla Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer over 1200 meters of ascent, ideal for challenging training rides.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Carrocera?
A: While specific seasonal conditions are not detailed, the region's diverse terrain suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in flatter areas, while mountainous sections might be cooler. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any historical sites or religious buildings along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You might come across sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the local heritage and spiritual significance.

Q: Are there any less trafficked roads for a more peaceful cycling experience?
A: Many of the well-maintained asphalt roads in the Carrocera area are less trafficked, especially when avoiding major national routes. This allows for a more tranquil and immersive cycling experience through the valleys, forests, and cultivated hills.
tips: Riello is a small town in the province of León, located next to the Omaña River, in the Western Leonesa Mountains. Like many of the settlements in this mineral-rich area, it goes back to the Bronze Age and the Roman exploitation of gold.
La Garandilla is a small town located at the confluence of the Omaña and Valdesamario rivers.

Carrizo de la Ribera is a small town located on the banks of the Órbigo river. Like many settlements on the Iberian Peninsula, its origin dates back to Celtic times. The most notable monument is the monastery of Santa María de Carrizo from the 12th century.

Tapia de la Ribera is a small town in the Luna River valley on the banks of the Selga de Ordás reservoir. Near the river, on the outskirts of the village, are the ruins of a 14th century castle of which only the watchtower remains.
